title: Memory and Forgetting as Irreversible Trace Regulation
one_sentence: This module defines memory and forgetting as the minimal trace regulation mechanism required for persistence under irreversibility.

core_compression.forgetting: compression/pruning that preserves directional constraints while reopening feasible path space

core_tests.memory_needed: Would failure to re-enter this trace cause catastrophic repeat?
core_tests.forgetting_needed: Would re-entering all traces collapse feasible path space?
core_tests.adaptive_persistence: Repeat-risk remains below threshold while feasible path set remains non-collapsed.

negative_constraints: Do not reduce memory to storage.
Do not reduce forgetting to deletion.
Do not treat this module as a vector database design.
